title: pinguicula pumila
intro: Pinguicula pumila, also known as the dwarf butterwort, is a small, carnivorous plant species native to Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pinguicula pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Pinguicula pumila can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by using the plant's extracts in topical creams and ointments.
